title: rubus × trachycladus
intro: Rubus × trachycladus, also known as the raspberry rose or Himalayan raspberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus and Rubus thibetanus. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. The health benefits of Rubus × trachycladus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Rubus × trachycladus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in jams and preserves due to its sweet-tart flavor and nutritional value.
